goal: Design or implement a safe runtime action boundary.
whatWasImplemented: Added a dry-run runtime action boundary with reload, keyword, dispatch, and status action types.
Added a mock executor that records intent and never executes real commands.
Added runtime action policy scaffolding that keeps all real execution disabled.
Added a runtime action review workflow that combines policy, dry-run result, and execution log while real_command_executed remains false.
Added tests proving mutating runtime actions remain dry-run only.
Kept real reload and mutating hyprctl outside active Apply behavior.
Added runtime command risk classification for status, reload, keyword, and dispatch actions.
Added deterministic tests proving status is read-only while reload, keyword, and dispatch are mutating classes that remain dry-run only.
No additional production-safe runtime/reload step was available this sprint without explicit runtime mutation approval.
This sprint rechecked runtime/reload and found no safe production step without explicit runtime mutation approval.
Controlled live-test guard requires read-only runtime snapshot before runtime mutation.

Added a guarded runtime executor model that supports read-only status proof, mutation dry-run proof, restore-command generation from prior snapshots, command logging, and default real_command_executed=false.
Attempted read-only hyprctl version and monitors queries; both failed without mutation because the runtime socket was unavailable in this shell.

Retried read-only runtime evidence and found /usr/bin/hyprctl, a Hyprland instance signature, and a socket directory under XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/hypr, but hyprctl version, monitors -j, and getoption general:gaps_in still failed without mutation with socket timeout.

Added a default-disabled runtime/reload production gate review requiring read-only evidence, prior value snapshot, restore command, command-specific recovery plan for dispatch, explicit approval, and production flag.
Connected runtime keyword/reload reviews to the shared explicit approval-flow model; live restore proof can reach ready_but_default_disabled in model tests, never production enabled by default.

Added runtime live-restore proof model for blocked read-only evidence, ready-but-default-disabled restore plans, and simulated live-restore-proven state; real-session read-only evidence now succeeds outside the sandbox.

Diagnosed the original socket timeout as sandbox permission/process isolation: direct socket connect failed with Operation not permitted inside the sandbox, while outside-sandbox hyprctl read-only queries succeeded.

Attempted one guarded low-risk general:gaps_in live-restore proof after preparing restore commands. hyprctl keyword was rejected for non-legacy parsers and tested assignment eval syntax failed before value change; the active Lua config syntax identified hyprctl eval 'hl.config({ general = { gaps_in = 6 } })' as the correct mutation form.
Ran the controlled hl.config eval proof outside the sandbox: gaps_in changed from 5 to 6, then the prepared restore command returned it to 5 and post-restore readback verified restoration.

Connected the proven hl.config eval live-restore proof and mutation syntax evidence to a default-disabled runtime approval review that can reach approved_but_default_disabled with valid approval token evidence.

Added tests proving missing proof, failed restore proof, wrong setting, wrong restore command, missing approval, wrong scope, expired approval, and valid approval behavior while production runtime/reload remains disabled.
whatWasNotImplemented: No real hyprctl keyword, dispatch, reload, or runtime mutation path was added.
No production runtime adapter was enabled.
blockers: Needs a production command approval model before any runtime adapter can be activated.
Production reload requires separate approval, read-only evidence, prior snapshot, restore command, and recovery proof.
Production runtime mutation remains disabled even though the low-risk live restore proof and approval review succeeded.
Reload and dispatch still require separate restore/recovery plans before any production activation.
nextRecommendedWork: Add a disabled runtime approval UI surface that displays the proven hl.config eval live-restore evidence without enabling production runtime/reload.
